en.content:
The United Methodist University of Mozambique in Cambine, Inhambane Province, graduated its first 76 students on Friday morning (06/17) in various fields of knowledge, including Theology, Education Sciences, Administration and Management, Engineering and Informatics, and Pedagogy and Didactics.

Of the 76 graduates, 34 hold master's degrees and 42 hold bachelor's degrees.

On the occasion, Ludmila Maguni, Secretary of State in Inhambane Province, challenged the newly graduated professionals to apply the knowledge acquired during their training to seek tangible solutions that contribute to resolving the problems affecting communities.

Maguni also urged the graduates to uphold responsibility, dedication, professional ethics, and commitment in building the country.

In her speech, she emphasized the need for the new graduates, in the new phase they will follow in the job market, to contribute to implementing the plans set by the Government at different levels of development.

For his part, UMUM Rector Júlio André Vilankulos stated that several actions are underway to improve, expand, and bring quality education closer to citizens.

Vilankulos also urged the trained professionals to make use of the knowledge acquired by putting it into practice.
en.summary: UMUM graduated 76 students in theology, education, management, engineering, and other fields.

fr.content:
L'Université Méthodiste Unie du Mozambique à Cambine, province d'Inhambane, a diplômé vendredi matin (17/06) ses 76 premiers étudiants dans divers domaines, notamment la théologie, les sciences de l'éducation, l'administration et la gestion, l'ingénierie et l'informatique, la pédagogie et la didactique.

Sur les 76 diplômés, 34 sont titulaires d'un master et 42 d'une licence.

À cette occasion, Ludmila Maguni, secrétaire d'État dans la province d'Inhambane, a exhorté les nouveaux diplômés à appliquer les connaissances acquises pour trouver des solutions tangibles aux problèmes des communautés.

Maguni a également appelé les étudiants à faire preuve de responsabilité, de dévouement, d'éthique professionnelle et d'engagement dans la construction du pays.

Dans son discours, elle a souligné la nécessité pour les nouveaux diplômés de contribuer à la mise en œuvre des plans du gouvernement aux différents niveaux de développement.

De son côté, le recteur de l'UMUM, Júlio André Vilankulos, a indiqué que plusieurs actions sont en cours pour améliorer, étendre et rapprocher un enseignement de qualité des citoyens.

Vilankulos a également exhorté les professionnels formés à mettre en pratique les connaissances acquises.
fr.summary: L'UMUM a diplômé 76 étudiants en théologie, éducation, gestion, ingénierie et autres domaines.

en.content:
November 10, 2022

World Science Day for Peace and Development has been celebrated worldwide since 2001, established by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) to reaffirm the role of science in building a better world.

This year, the Central Ceremonies in the country took place at the United Methodist University of Mozambique, specifically at the Cambine University Campus in Morrumbene district, under the theme "BASIC SCIENCES FOR SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT."

The celebrations were presided over by the National Director of Science, Technology and Higher Education, Dr. Eugênia Cossa, representing the Ministry of Science, Technology and Higher Education, who in her address emphasized that the purpose of this day is to keep citizens well informed about scientific discoveries and advances.

Present were PhD. Marcos Chirinda, UNESCO representative in Mozambique, accompanied by Dr. Amosse Macamo, representative of the Provincial Secretary of State of Inhambane, the Rector of UMUM, PhD. Júlio André Vilanculos, researchers, guests, and the UMUM academic community.

It was a ceremony full of debates and reflections. Associated with this day, the II Entrepreneurship Fair was held simultaneously, organized by the Faculty of Administration, Management and Accounting Sciences of UMUM under the theme "Inter-relating education and business for the country's economic and social development." Exhibitors included higher education institutions, entrepreneurs, innovators, and informal market vendors.

This day was an opportunity to mobilize all sectors of society around the theme, from government to educational institutions and Mozambican researchers.
en.summary: Central ceremonies for World Science Day and the II Entrepreneurship Fair were held at UMUM in Cambine.

fr.content:
10 novembre 2022

La Journée mondiale de la science pour la paix et le développement est célébrée dans le monde entier depuis 2001, établie par l'Organisation des Nations Unies pour l'éducation, la science et la culture (UNESCO) pour réaffirmer le rôle de la science dans la construction d'un monde meilleur.

Cette année, les cérémonies centrales dans le pays ont eu lieu à l'Université Méthodiste Unie du Mozambique, sur le campus universitaire de Cambine, dans le district de Morrumbene, sous le thème « SCIENCES FONDAMENTALES POUR LE DÉVELOPPEMENT DURABLE ».

Les célébrations ont été présidées par la directrice nationale de la science, de la technologie et de l'enseignement supérieur, Dr. Eugênia Cossa, représentant le ministère, qui a souligné que l'objectif de cette journée est de tenir les citoyens informés des découvertes et avancées scientifiques.

Étaient présents le PhD. Marcos Chirinda, représentant de l'UNESCO au Mozambique, le Dr. Amosse Macamo, représentant de la secrétaire d'État provinciale d'Inhambane, le recteur de l'UMUM, PhD. Júlio André Vilanculos, des chercheurs, des invités et la communauté académique de l'UMUM.

La cérémonie a été riche en débats et réflexions. Parallèlement, la IIe foire de l'entrepreneuriat a été organisée par la Faculté des sciences de l'administration, de la gestion et de la comptabilité de l'UMUM sous le thème « Relier l'enseignement et les affaires pour le développement économique et social du pays ».

Cette journée a été l'occasion de mobiliser tous les acteurs de la société autour du thème, du gouvernement aux institutions d'enseignement et aux chercheurs mozambicains.
fr.summary: Cérémonies centrales de la Journée mondiale de la science et IIe foire de l'entrepreneuriat à l'UMUM de Cambine.

en.content:
The United Methodist University of Mozambique (UMUM) received a new Rector following a selection process conducted by the United Methodist Church in Mozambique, its founding institution.

Prof. Dr. Sosdito Estevão Mananze was selected and officially inaugurated on April 16, 2024, at the UMUM University Campus in Cambine. The inauguration ceremony was marked by significant symbolism and was praised by participants.

During his speech, the new Rector highlighted the importance of uniting the entire academic community around common goals.

In addition to UMUM members, the ceremony was attended by the local Methodist community, superintendents, and pastors.

The ceremony was presided over by Reverend Massango, Superintendent of one of the Ecclesiastical Districts, on behalf of the Bishop, who was unable to attend due to health issues.

The leadership transition at UMUM represents a moment of renewal and commitment to academic excellence and service to the community.
en.summary: UMUM inaugurated Prof. Dr. Sosdito Estevão Mananze as its new Rector at the Cambine campus.

fr.content:
L'Université Méthodiste Unie du Mozambique (UMUM) a reçu un nouveau recteur à l'issue d'un processus de sélection mené par l'Église Méthodiste Unie du Mozambique, son institution fondatrice.

Le Prof. Dr. Sosdito Estevão Mananze a été sélectionné et officiellement investi le 16 avril 2024, sur le campus universitaire de l'UMUM à Cambine. La cérémonie d'investiture a été marquée par un symbolisme important et saluée par les participants.

Dans son discours, le nouveau recteur a souligné l'importance d'unir toute la communauté académique autour d'objectifs communs.

Outre les membres de l'UMUM, la cérémonie a réuni la communauté méthodiste locale, des surintendants et des pasteurs.

La cérémonie a été présidée par le révérend Massango, surintendant de l'un des districts ecclésiastiques, au nom de l'évêque, qui n'a pas pu être présent pour des raisons de santé.

La transition de leadership à l'UMUM représente un moment de renouveau et d'engagement envers l'excellence académique et le service à la communauté.
fr.summary: L'UMUM a investi le Prof. Dr. Sosdito Estevão Mananze comme nouveau recteur sur le campus de Cambine.

en.content:
With great sadness, the General Board of Higher Education and Ministry (GBHEM) learned of the passing of Rev. Prof. Dr. Kongolo Clement Chijika, President of the United Methodist University of Katanga in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and former President of the Association of African Methodist Institutions of Higher Education (AAMIHE). He passed away on Friday, April 12, 2024, after a brief battle with cancer and hospitalization in South Africa. His death came as a shock and prompted expressions of condolence and solidarity around the world.

Kongolo Chijika was a well-known Methodist leader and educator in Africa. He studied in his homeland, the DRC, received his theological training at the Mulungwishi Mission of the United Methodist Church, and then pursued postgraduate studies in Canada, where he earned his PhD in Theology. He returned to the DRC and to his alma mater, where he began a career as a scholar, professor of the Old Testament, Dean of the Faculty of Theology, and later President, following in the footsteps of Bishop Kasap Tshibang Owan after his election as Bishop and departure from university leadership.

Kongolo Chijika was involved in many United Methodist educational projects in Africa and around the world. He was a leader in the Association of African United Methodist Theological Institutions (AAUMTI) when he was Dean of his theological school, helped create the Association of African Methodist Institutions of Higher Education (AAMIHE), and served as its second President between 2019 and 2023. He was a member of the Board of Trustees of Africa University and served on the Central Conference Theological Education Fund (CCTEF) Commission.

He also participated in the World Forum promoted by the International Association of Methodist Schools, Colleges and Universities (IAMSCU) and was active in various projects, colloquia, and events organized by GBHEM. He was involved not only in the DRC and many other African countries, but also in the United States and other parts of the world. This included his involvement in international conferences in Japan, Mexico, and the United Kingdom, where he led the delegation of African participants at the IAMSCU-MSN Joint Conference "Transforming Lives," which celebrated the 275th anniversary of the founding of Kingswood School by John Wesley in 1748.

Professor Kongolo was deeply involved in both theological education and higher education. He was also an elected delegate to several United Methodist conferences, including Annual Conferences, Central Conferences, and several General Conference sessions. As an official delegate of the United Methodist Church of Southern Congo to General Conference 2020, now held in Charlotte, NC, USA, in 2024, he was very active in preparing new legislation, including the petition for the creation of a Central Conference Higher Education Fund (CCHEF) to complement the existing CCTEF.

Rev. Prof. Dr. Kongolo Clement Chijika was a great partner and friend of GBHEM, a true United Methodist leader, and a talented African educator. With his passing, he leaves a great void in his homeland, his university, and his Church in the DRC and around the world. In this hour of loss and mourning, we celebrate his life and legacy, and express our deepest condolences to his family and community.
en.summary: GBHEM mourns the passing of Rev. Prof. Dr. Kongolo Clement Chijika, former AAMIHE President and leader in African Methodist higher education.

fr.content:
C'est avec une grande tristesse que la Commission générale de l'éducation supérieure et du ministère (GBHEM) a appris le décès du Rev. Prof. Dr. Kongolo Clement Chijika, président de l'Université Méthodiste Unie du Katanga en République Démocratique du Congo (RDC) et ancien président de l'Association Africaine des Institutions Méthodistes d'Enseignement Supérieur (AAMIHE). Il est décédé le vendredi 12 avril 2024, après une brève bataille contre le cancer et une hospitalisation en Afrique du Sud. Sa mort a été un choc et a suscité des messages de condoléances et de solidarité dans le monde entier.

Kongolo Chijika était un leader méthodiste et un éducateur très connu en Afrique. Il a étudié dans son pays natal, la RDC, a reçu sa formation théologique à la Mission de Mulungwishi de l'Église Méthodiste Unie, puis a poursuivi ses études supérieures au Canada, où il a obtenu son doctorat en théologie. Il est retourné en RDC et dans son alma mater, où il a commencé une carrière comme chercheur, professeur de l'Ancien Testament, doyen de la Faculté de Théologie, puis président, suivant les traces de l'évêque Kasap Tshibang Owan après son élection comme évêque et son départ de la direction de l'université.

Kongolo Chijika a participé à de nombreux projets éducatifs méthodistes unis en Afrique et dans le monde. Il a été leader de l'Association Africaine des Institutions Théologiques Méthodistes Unies (AAUMTI) lorsqu'il était doyen de son école de théologie, a contribué à la création de l'AAMIHE et en a été le deuxième président entre 2019 et 2023. Il était membre du conseil d'administration de l'Université de l'Afrique et de la commission du Fonds d'éducation théologique de la Conférence centrale (CCTEF).

Il a également participé au Forum mondial promu par l'Association Internationale des Écoles, Collèges et Universités Méthodistes (IAMSCU) et a été actif dans divers projets, colloques et événements organisés par la GBHEM. Il était impliqué non seulement en RDC et dans de nombreux autres pays africains, mais aussi aux États-Unis et dans d'autres parties du monde, y compris des conférences internationales au Japon, au Mexique et au Royaume-Uni, où il a dirigé la délégation africaine à la conférence conjointe IAMSCU-MSN « Transforming Lives », célébrant le 275e anniversaire de la fondation de Kingswood School par John Wesley en 1748.

Le professeur Kongolo était profondément impliqué dans l'éducation théologique et l'enseignement supérieur. Il a également été délégué élu à plusieurs conférences méthodistes unies. En tant que délégué officiel de l'Église Méthodiste Unie du Congo du Sud à la Conférence générale 2020, tenue à Charlotte, NC, États-Unis, en 2024, il a été très actif dans la préparation de nouvelles législations, y compris la pétition pour la création d'un Fonds d'éducation supérieure de la Conférence centrale (CCHEF) pour compléter le CCTEF existant.

Le Rev. Prof. Dr. Kongolo Clement Chijika était un grand partenaire et ami de la GBHEM, un véritable leader méthodiste unie et un éducateur africain talentueux. Par son décès, il laisse un grand vide dans son pays natal, son université et son Église en RDC et dans le monde. En cette heure de deuil, nous célébrons sa vie et son héritage, et exprimons nos plus sincères condoléances à sa famille et à sa communauté.
fr.summary: La GBHEM rend hommage au Rev. Prof. Dr. Kongolo Clement Chijika, ancien président de l'AAMIHE et leader de l'enseignement supérieur méthodiste en Afrique.

pt.content:
O Nyadire Teacher's College demonstra talento e abraça a Educação 5.0

Nyadire, Mutoko – Num vibrante demonstração de excelência académica e espírito inovador, o Nyadire Teacher's College tem destacado-se em Zimbabwe com exposições que reflectem o talento da instituição e a adopção integral da Educação 5.0.

À medida que o país transita para o modelo transformador da Educação 5.0, o colégio emergiu como pioneiro, integrando os cinco pilares: ensino, investigação, envolvimento comunitário, inovação e industrialização.

«Estamos empenhados em formar diplomados completos, com conhecimentos teóricos e competências práticas para prosperar no século XXI», afirmou a directora Mrs Nyarugwe.

As salas de aula transformaram-se em centros de criatividade, com metodologias activas que desenvolvem pensamento crítico, resolução de problemas e trabalho colaborativo.
pt.summary: Nyadire Teacher's College destaca-se em Zimbabwe com projectos alinhados à Educação 5.0.

fr.content:
Le Nyadire Teacher's College met en valeur ses talents et adopte l'Éducation 5.0

Nyadire, Mutoko – Dans une démonstration vibrante de prouesse académique et d'esprit innovant, le Nyadire Teacher's College fait des vagues au Zimbabwe avec des expositions reflétant le talent de l'institution et son adoption de l'Éducation 5.0.

Alors que le pays passe au modèle transformateur de l'Éducation 5.0, le collège est devenu un pionnier, intégrant les cinq piliers : enseignement, recherche, engagement communautaire, innovation et industrialisation.

« Nous nous engageons à former des diplômés complets, dotés de connaissances théoriques et de compétences pratiques », déclare la directrice Mrs Nyarugwe.
fr.summary: Le Nyadire Teacher's College se distingue au Zimbabwe avec des projets alignés sur l'Éducation 5.0.

en.content:
Nyadire Teacher's College Showcases Talent and Embraces Education 5.0

Nyadire, Mutoko - In a vibrant display of academic prowess and innovative spirit, Nyadire Teacher's College has been making waves across Zimbabwe with its multifaceted exhibitions reflecting the institution's talent and its whole-hearted embrace of Education 5.0.

As the country transitions towards the transformative model of Education 5.0, Nyadire Teacher's College has emerged as a trailblazer, seamlessly integrating the five key pillars of this paradigm shift - teaching, research, community engagement, innovation, and industrialization.

"We are committed to producing well-rounded graduates who are not only equipped with theoretical knowledge but also possess the practical skills and entrepreneurial mindset necessary to thrive in the 21st century," says Mrs Nyarugwe, the Principal of Nyadire Teacher's College.

"Education 5.0 is the cornerstone of our institutional vision, and we are proud to showcase our progress in this transformative journey."

One of the standout features of Nyadire's exhibitions has been the innovative teaching methodologies employed by the college's Arts section. Through interactive demonstrations, simulations, and hands-on workshops, students are actively engaged in the learning process, developing critical thinking, problem-solving, and collaborative skills.

"Our classrooms have been transformed into hubs of creativity and exploration," explains Mrs Nyarugwe. "We encourage our students to step out of their comfort zones, experiment with new ideas, and apply their knowledge to real-world challenges. This approach not only enhances their academic performance but also prepares them to be agents of change in their communities."

Nyadire's commitment to research and community engagement has also been a major highlight of its exhibitions. Students and teachers have showcased their groundbreaking research projects, ranging from educational interventions to sustainable development initiatives, all of which address Heritage-based pressing issues faced by local communities.

"We believe that education should not be confined within the walls of the classroom," says Mrs Nyarugwe. "Our students are actively involved in community-based projects, where they apply their knowledge and skills to solve real-world problems. This not only enriches their learning experience but also fosters a sense of social responsibility and civic engagement."

The college's emphasis on innovation and industrialization has also been on full display, with students demonstrating their entrepreneurial skills and showcasing their cutting-edge technological solutions. From creative prototypes to digital learning platforms, Nyadire's students have proven their ability to blend academic rigor with practical innovation.

"Education 5.0 is not just about imparting knowledge; it's about empowering our students to be agents of transformation," concludes the Principal. "As we continue to exhibit our talents and achievements, we hope to inspire other institutions to embrace this holistic approach to education and join us in shaping the future of Zimbabwe."

en.summary: Nyadire Teacher's College Showcases Talent and Embraces Education 5.0 Nyadire, Mutoko - In a vibrant display of academic prowess and innovative spirit, Nyadire Teacher's College has been making waves

pt.content:
MONROVIA – Iniciou hoje uma formação de uma semana sobre acidificação dos oceanos, reunindo participantes da Libéria, Gana, Costa do Marfim e outras nações da África Ocidental. A formação, realizada no Hotel Belle Casa em Monróvia de 9 a 13 de setembro de 2024, visa reforçar o conhecimento científico sobre acidificação dos oceanos entre cientistas em início de carreira na região.

Este programa é um esforço conjunto entre o Governo da Libéria (GoL), a Universidade Metodista Unida (UMU) e a Agência de Proteção Ambiental (EPA) da Libéria, com apoio adicional de parceiros internacionais, incluindo o Centro Internacional de Coordenação sobre Acidificação dos Oceanos (OA-ICC), a rede Global Ocean Acidification Africa (OA-Africa), a Aliança Internacional para Combater a Acidificação dos Oceanos (OA-Alliance) e o projecto Empowering West African Scientists for a Sustainable Future.

Na cerimónia de abertura, o director executivo da EPA, Dr. Emmanuel Urey Yarkpawolo, reafirmou o empenho da agência na aplicação das leis ambientais da Libéria e no cumprimento das obrigações internacionais sob a administração do Presidente Joseph Boakai.

A acidificação dos oceanos, causada pela absorção de excesso de dióxido de carbono (CO2) pela atmosfera, é foco central desta formação, representando riscos para a biodiversidade marinha e para economias dependentes dos recursos marinhos, como a Libéria.

A presidente da UMU, Dr. Yar-Donlah Gonway Gono, sublinhou o compromisso da universidade em desenvolver soluções inovadoras para desafios complexos como a acidificação dos oceanos e anunciou o lançamento da Rede de Acidificação dos Oceanos do Golfo da Guiné durante a formação.
pt.summary: Formação regional sobre acidificação dos oceanos reúne cientistas da África Ocidental em Monróvia.

fr.content:
MONROVIA – Une formation d'une semaine sur l'acidification des océans a débuté aujourd'hui, réunissant des participants du Liberia, du Ghana, de la Côte d'Ivoire et d'autres nations d'Afrique de l'Ouest. La formation, organisée à l'hôtel Belle Casa à Monrovia du 9 au 13 septembre 2024, vise à renforcer les connaissances scientifiques sur l'acidification des océans parmi les jeunes chercheurs de la région.

Ce programme est un effort conjoint du gouvernement du Liberia (GoL), de l'Université Méthodiste Unie (UMU) et de l'Agence de protection de l'environnement (EPA) du Liberia, avec le soutien de partenaires internationaux, notamment le Centre international de coordination sur l'acidification des océans (OA-ICC), le réseau OA-Africa et l'Alliance internationale pour combattre l'acidification des océans (OA-Alliance).

Lors de la cérémonie d'ouverture, le directeur exécutif de l'EPA, Dr. Emmanuel Urey Yarkpawolo, a réaffirmé l'engagement de l'agence à appliquer les lois environnementales du Liberia et à respecter les obligations internationales.

L'acidification des océans, causée par l'absorption du excès de CO2 atmosphérique, menace la biodiversité marine et les économies dépendantes des ressources marines, notamment au Liberia.

La présidente de l'UMU, Dr. Yar-Donlah Gonway Gono, a salué l'organisation de l'événement et annoncé le lancement du réseau d'acidification des océans du golfe de Guinée, décrivant ce moment comme « pivot » pour la région.
fr.summary: Formation régionale sur l'acidification des océans à Monrovia pour les scientifiques d'Afrique de l'Ouest.

en.content:
MONROVIA – A week-long training on ocean acidification commenced today, gathering participants from Liberia, Ghana, Ivory Coast, and other West African nations. The training, hosted at the Belle Casa Hotel in Monrovia from September 9-13, 2024, aims to boost scientific knowledge on ocean acidification among early-career scientists across the region.

This program is a collaborative effort between the Government of Liberia (GoL), the United Methodist University (UMU), and the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) of Liberia, with additional support from international partners, including the Ocean Acidification International Coordination Centre (OA-ICC), the Global Ocean Acidification Africa (OA-Africa) network, the International Alliance to Combat Ocean Acidification (OA-Alliance), and Empowering West African Scientists for a Sustainable Future.

Speaking at the opening ceremony, EPA Executive Director Dr. Emmanuel Urey Yarkpawolo reaffirmed the agency’s dedication to enforcing Liberia’s environmental laws and fulfilling international obligations under President Joseph Boakai’s administration.

“Part of our enforcement has to do with fulfilling international obligations,” Dr. Urey Yarkpawolo stated, highlighting the significance of global collaboration in tackling environmental challenges like ocean acidification.

He mentioned the EPA’s recent training on basic occupational radiation safety and the agency’s ongoing efforts to promote environmental safety in Liberia. Dr. Urey Yarkpawolo also revealed plans to travel to Vienna, Austria, alongside EPA’s Rafael Ngunbu and officials from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, to deposit key international agreements on nuclear safety.

“Ocean acidification, caused by the ocean’s absorption of excess carbon dioxide (CO2) from the atmosphere, is a key focus of this training,” Dr. Urey Yarkpawolo explained, noting that it threatens marine biodiversity and poses socio-economic risks, especially for nations like Liberia that rely heavily on marine resources.

He urged participants to use this opportunity to collaborate and strengthen regional efforts to combat climate change, food insecurity, and environmental health challenges.

UMU President Dr. Yar-Donlah Gonway Gono also addressed the gathering, emphasizing the importance of the training in addressing one of the most urgent environmental issues of our time.

“At the United Methodist University, we are deeply committed to advancing knowledge that serves not only the academic community but society at large,” Dr. Gono said, highlighting UMU’s commitment to developing innovative solutions to complex issues like ocean acidification.

Dr. Gono commended UMU’s Dean of Natural and Applied Sciences, Mr. Sheck Sheriff, for his leadership in organizing the event and thanked the OA-ICC and other sponsors for supporting the training.

Throughout the week, participants will engage in lectures, workshops, and fieldwork aimed at equipping them with the tools to monitor and mitigate the impacts of ocean acidification. Dr. Gono also announced the launch of the Gulf of Guinea Ocean Acidification Network during the training, describing it as a “pivotal moment” for the region.

“The network will bring together talents, knowledge, and resources to advance collective scientific efforts in addressing ocean acidification, further strengthening regional collaboration,” she added.

Representatives from the OA-ICC, including Ms. Lina Hansson and Mr. Samuel Dupont, expressed their appreciation to the Liberian government, the EPA, UMU, and other partners for organizing the event. They encouraged participants to take the training seriously, emphasizing the vital role scientists will play in addressing ocean acidification in their respective countries.

The support of international bodies like the OA-ICC, alongside contributions from the OA-Alliance, has been crucial in ensuring that West African nations can improve their understanding and response to ocean acidification.
en.summary: MONROVIA –  A week-long training on ocean acidification commenced today, gathering participants from Liberia, Ghana, Ivory Coast, and other West African nations. The training, hosted at the Belle
